name: Lyndhurst
overview: Lyndhurst is the largest village in the New Forest and its 'capital', a civil parish in the New Forest National Park about nine miles south-west of Southampton, with a population of around 3,029 (2011 census). It became the administrative heart of the Forest after William the Conqueror made it a royal hunting ground, and the Queen's/King's House at the top of the High Street still holds the ancient Verderers' Court and the Forestry Commission headquarters. The Gothic church of St Michael and All Angels has a 49-metre spire and the grave of Alice Liddell, inspiration for Alice in Wonderland.
character: A bustling, tourist-focused New Forest village with a busy independent High Street, surrounded by open forest where ponies roam, and steeped in royal and literary history.
amenities: Long High Street of independent shops, boutiques and galleries
Many pubs, cafes, tea rooms, restaurants and hotels
New Forest Heritage Centre and Museum (Visitor Information Centre)

schools.name: Secondary schools in the New Forest (e.g. Brockenhurst College for sixth form)

commutes.notes: Via Brockenhurst or Ashurst (New Forest) stations

prices.summary: A premium New Forest location; period and character homes near open forest command high values, supported by tourism, amenities and the National Park setting. See Rightmove for current sold-price detail.

thingsToDo: New Forest Heritage Centre and Museum
St Michael and All Angels Church and Alice Liddell's grave
Walks from Bolton's Bench into the open forest
Shopping and dining on the High Street
Pony-spotting and cycling in the National Park
placesToEat: High Street pubs, cafes and tea rooms
Numerous restaurants and the nearby Bank/Emery Down/Swan Green pubs
pros: Largest village in the New Forest with full amenities and a supermarket
Surrounded by open forest and superb walking/cycling
Rich history (Verderers' Court, royal connections, Alice Liddell)
cons: No railway station in the village (Brockenhurst/Ashurst nearest)
Heavy tourist traffic and parking pressure in season
whoItSuits: Buyers wanting a lively, amenity-rich New Forest village at the heart of the National Park, who accept tourist bustle and premium prices.
